fundamentals.name: Applied Materials, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Materials, Inc. provides materials engineering solutions, equipment, services, and software to the semiconductor and related industries in the United States, China, Korea, Taiwan, Japan, Southeast Asia, Europe, and internationally. The company operates through Semiconductor Systems and Applied Global Services (AGS) segments. The Semiconductor Systems segment includes semiconductor capital equipment to enable materials engineering steps, including etch, rapid thermal processing, deposition, chemical mechanical planarization, metrology and inspection, wafer packaging, and ion implantation. The AGS segment offers integrated solutions to optimize equipment and fab performance and productivity comprising spares, upgrades, services, and 200 millimeter and other equipment and factory automation software for semiconductor and other products. It serves manufacturers of semiconductor wafers and chips, and other electronic devices. Applied Materials, Inc. was incorporated in 1967 and is headquartered in Santa Clara, California.

judgment.summary: A high-growth, highly profitable company with strong margins and a solid balance sheet, trading at a reasonable forward multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 6.5% with EPS of $2.86 vs $2.69. Next earnings date is 2026-08-13.
positives: Revenue growth 11.4% YoY and earnings growth 33.5% YoY indicate strong business expansion.
Profit margin 29.3% and return on equity 39.7% demonstrate excellent profitability and efficiency.
Forward P/E of 27.81 is attractive given the high growth and profitability.
Strong free cash flow of $3.04B supports operations and shareholder returns.
Analyst consensus is 'strong buy' with a mean target of $511.17, suggesting significant upside potential.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 42.39 is high, though justified by growth.
Beta of 1.65 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Applied Materials (AMAT) has seen significant positive news flow following its blowout February earnings beat, which included a 31.3% quarterly earnings growth and an EPS surprise of 6.5%. The company has also announced new AI chip equipment partnerships, leading to an analyst raising their target to $500 and a consensus 'strong buy' rating. This strong performance has positioned AMAT as a top AI semiconductor stock, outperforming the Nasdaq.
